title: Gyárfás' Complementation Conjecture (Conjecture 6.3)
statement_text: Let $c \geq 0$, and let $\mathcal{C}$ be a class of graphs such that $\chi(H) \leq \omega(H) + c$ for all $G \in \mathcal{C}$ and every induced subgraph $H$ of $G$. Let $\mathcal{C}'$ be the class of complements of members of $\mathcal{C}$. Then $\mathcal{C}'$ is $\chi$-bounded.
context_text: In 1987, Gyárfás conjectured (conjecture 6.3 of [4]) that for all $c$, the function $f(x) = x + c$ has a complementary bounding function. Gyárfás had proved that if $f$ has a complementary bounding function then $\inf f(x)/x = 1$, making $f(x) = x + c$ the natural boundary case. This is the central subject of the paper, stated as Theorem 1.1.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full; more generally, establishes (Theorem 1.2) that a class of graphs is $\chi$-bounded if no graph in the class contains $c+1$ odd holes pairwise anticomplete, from which the conjecture follows by applying this to complements.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VII. Gyárfás' complementation conjecture

title: Gyárfás Conjecture 6.8
statement_text: Let $\mathcal{C}$ be the class of graphs $G$ such that $\alpha(H)\omega(H) \geq |V(H)| - 1$ for every induced subgraph $H$ of $G$. Then $\mathcal{C}$ is $\chi$-bounded.
context_text: Vaidy Sivaraman observed (private communication) that Theorem 1.2 of the paper also implies conjecture 6.8 of Gyárfás [4]. The implication is immediate since if $H$ is the disjoint union of two odd cycles of length at least four, then $\alpha(H)\omega(H) = |V(H)| - 2$, so any graph in $\mathcal{C}$ cannot contain two pairwise anticomplete odd holes.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture as an immediate corollary of Theorem 1.2 (the $\chi$-boundedness result for classes with no $c+1$ pairwise anticomplete odd holes).
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VII. Gyárfás' complementation conjecture
